NKJV.1: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin that grace may abound?
NKJV.2: Certainly not! How shall we who died to sin live any longer in it?
NKJV.3: Or do you not know that as many of us as were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into His death?
NKJV.4: Therefore we were buried with Him through baptism into death, that just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, even so we also should walk in newness of life.
NKJV.5: For if we have been united together in the likeness of His death, certainly we also shall be in the likeness of His resurrection,
NKJV.6: knowing this, that our old man was crucified with Him, that the body of sin might be done away with, that we should no longer be slaves of sin.
NKJV.7: For he who has died has been freed from sin.
NKJV.8: Now if we died with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with Him,
NKJV.9: knowing that Christ, having been raised from the dead, dies no more. Death no longer has dominion over Him.
NKJV.10: For the death that He died, He died to sin once for all; but the life that He lives, He lives to God.
NKJV.11: Likewise you also, reckon yourselves to be dead indeed to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
NKJV.12: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body, that you should obey it in its lusts.
NKJV.13: And do not present your members as instruments of unrighteousness to sin, but present yourselves to God as being alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness to God.
NKJV.14: For sin shall not have dominion over you, for you are not under law but under grace.
NKJV.15: What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? Certainly not!
NKJV.16: Do you not know that to whom you present yourselves slaves to obey, you are that one’s slaves whom you obey, whether of sin leading to death, or of obedience leading to righteousness?
NKJV.17: But God be thanked that though you were slaves of sin, yet you obeyed from the heart that form of doctrine to which you were delivered.
NKJV.18: And having been set free from sin, you became slaves of righteousness.
NKJV.19: I speak in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh. For just as you presented your members as slaves of uncleanness, and of lawlessness leading to more lawlessness, so now present your members as slaves of righteousness for holiness.
NKJV.20: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
NKJV.21: What fruit did you have then in the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.
NKJV.22: But now having been set free from sin, and having become slaves of God, you have your fruit to holiness, and the end, everlasting life.
NKJV.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

ESV.1: What shall we say then? Are we to continue in sin that grace may abound?
ESV.2: By no means! How can we who died to sin still live in it?
ESV.3: Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
ESV.4: We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death, in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life.
ESV.5: For if we have been united with him in a death like his, we shall certainly be united with him in a resurrection like his.
ESV.6: We know that our old self  was crucified with him in order that the body of sin might be brought to nothing, so that we would no longer be enslaved to sin.
ESV.7: For one who has died has been set free  from sin.
ESV.8: Now if we have died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
ESV.9: We know that Christ, being raised from the dead, will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him.
ESV.10: For the death he died he died to sin, once for all, but the life he lives he lives to God.
ESV.11: So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
ESV.12: Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, to make you obey its passions.
ESV.13: Do not present your members to sin as instruments for unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life, and your members to God as instruments for righteousness.
ESV.14: For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace.
ESV.15: What then? Are we to sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!
ESV.16: Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves,  you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness?
ESV.17: But thanks be to God, that you who were once slaves of sin have become obedient from the heart to the standard of teaching to which you were committed,
ESV.18: and, having been set free from sin, have become slaves of righteousness.
ESV.19: I am speaking in human terms, because of your natural limitations. For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and to lawlessness leading to more lawlessness, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness leading to sanctification.
ESV.20: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
ESV.21: But what fruit were you getting at that time from the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.
ESV.22: But now that you have been set free from sin and have become slaves of God, the fruit you get leads to sanctification and its end, eternal life.
ESV.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

NASB.1: What shall we say then? Are we to continue in sin so that grace may increase?
NASB.2: May it never be! How shall we who died to sin still live in it?
NASB.3: Or do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus have been baptized into His death?
NASB.4: Therefore we have been buried with Him through baptism into death, so that as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, so we too might walk in newness of life.
NASB.5: For if we have become united with [Him] in the likeness of His death, certainly we shall also be [in the likeness] of His resurrection,
NASB.6: knowing this, that our old self was crucified with [Him], in order that our body of sin might be done away with, so that we would no longer be slaves to sin;
NASB.7: for he who has died is freed from sin.
NASB.8: Now if we have died with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with Him,
NASB.9: knowing that Christ, having been raised from the dead, is never to die again; death no longer is master over Him.
NASB.10: For the death that He died, He died to sin once for all; but the life that He lives, He lives to God.
NASB.11: Even so consider yourselves to be dead to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus.
NASB.12: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its lusts,
NASB.13: and do not go on presenting the members of your body to sin [as] instruments of unrighteousness; but present yourselves to God as those alive from the dead, and your members [as] instruments of righteousness to God.
NASB.14: For sin shall not be master over you, for you are not under law but under grace.
NASB.15: What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? May it never be!
NASB.16: Do you not know that when you present yourselves to someone [as] slaves for obedience, you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin resulting in death, or of obedience resulting in righteousness?
NASB.17: But thanks be to God that though you were slaves of sin, you became obedient from the heart to that form of teaching to which you were committed,
NASB.18: and having been freed from sin, you became slaves of righteousness.
NASB.19: I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh. For just as you presented your members as slaves to impurity and to lawlessness, resulting in [further] lawlessness, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness, resulting in sanctification.
NASB.20: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
NASB.21: Therefore what benefit were you then deriving from the things of which you are now ashamed? For the outcome of those things is death.
NASB.22: But now having been freed from sin and enslaved to God, you derive your benefit, resulting in sanctification, and the outcome, eternal life.
NASB.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

NLT.1: Well then, should we keep on sinning so that God can show us more and more of his wonderful grace?
NLT.2: Of course not! Since we have died to sin, how can we continue to live in it?
NLT.3: Or have you forgotten that when we were joined with Christ Jesus in baptism, we joined him in his death?
NLT.4: For we died and were buried with Christ by baptism. And just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glorious power of the Father, now we also may live new lives.
NLT.5: Since we have been united with him in his death, we will also be raised to life as he was.
NLT.6: We know that our old sinful selves were crucified with Christ so that sin might lose its power in our lives. We are no longer slaves to sin.
NLT.7: For when we died with Christ we were set free from the power of sin.
NLT.8: And since we died with Christ, we know we will also live with him.
NLT.9: We are sure of this because Christ was raised from the dead, and he will never die again. Death no longer has any power over him.
NLT.10: When he died, he died once to break the power of sin. But now that he lives, he lives for the glory of God.
NLT.11: So you also should consider yourselves to be dead to the power of sin and alive to God through Christ Jesus.
NLT.12: Do not let sin control the way you live; do not give in to sinful desires.
NLT.13: Do not let any part of your body become an instrument of evil to serve sin. Instead, give yourselves completely to God, for you were dead, but now you have new life. So use your whole body as an instrument to do what is right for the glory of God.
NLT.14: Sin is no longer your master, for you no longer live under the requirements of the law. Instead, you live under the freedom of God’s grace.
NLT.15: Well then, since God’s grace has set us free from the law, does that mean we can go on sinning? Of course not!
NLT.16: Don’t you realize that you become the slave of whatever you choose to obey? You can be a slave to sin, which leads to death, or you can choose to obey God, which leads to righteous living.
NLT.17: Thank God! Once you were slaves of sin, but now you wholeheartedly obey this teaching we have given you.
NLT.18: Now you are free from your slavery to sin, and you have become slaves to righteous living.
NLT.19: Because of the weakness of your human nature, I am using the illustration of slavery to help you understand all this. Previously, you let yourselves be slaves to impurity and lawlessness, which led ever deeper into sin. Now you must give yourselves to be slaves to righteous living so that you will become holy.
NLT.20: When you were slaves to sin, you were free from the obligation to do right.
NLT.21: And what was the result? You are now ashamed of the things you used to do, things that end in eternal doom.
NLT.22: But now you are free from the power of sin and have become slaves of God. Now you do those things that lead to holiness and result in eternal life.
NLT.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life through Christ Jesus our Lord.

WEB.1: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound?
WEB.2: May it never be! We who died to sin, how could we live in it any longer?
WEB.3: Or don’t you know that all we who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
WEB.4: We were buried therefore with him through baptism into death, that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, so we also might walk in newness of life.
WEB.5: For if we have become united with him in the likeness of his death, we will also be part of his resurrection;
WEB.6: knowing this, that our old man was crucified with him, that the body of sin might be done away with, so that we would no longer be in bondage to sin.
WEB.7: For he who has died has been freed from sin.
WEB.8: But if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him;
WEB.9: knowing that Christ, being raised from the dead, dies no more. Death no more has dominion over him!
WEB.10: For the death that he died, he died to sin one time; but the life that he lives, he lives to God.
WEB.11: Thus consider yourselves also to be dead to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
WEB.12: Therefore don’t let sin reign in your mortal body, that you should obey it in its lusts.
WEB.13: Also, do not present your members to sin as instruments of unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God, as alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness to God.
WEB.14: For sin will not have dominion over you. For you are not under law, but under grace.
WEB.15: What then? Shall we sin, because we are not under law, but under grace? May it never be!
WEB.16: Don’t you know that when you present yourselves as servants and obey someone, you are the servants of whomever you obey; whether of sin to death, or of obedience to righteousness?
WEB.17: But thanks be to God, that, whereas you were bondservants of sin, you became obedient from the heart to that form of teaching to which you were delivered.
WEB.18: Being made free from sin, you became bondservants of righteousness.
WEB.19: I speak in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh, for as you presented your members as servants to uncleanness and to wickedness upon wickedness, even so now present your members as servants to righteousness for sanctification.
WEB.20: For when you were servants of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
WEB.21: What fruit then did you have at that time in the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.
WEB.22: But now, being made free from sin, and having become servants of God, you have your fruit of sanctification, and the result of eternal life.
WEB.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

CSB17.1: What should we say then? <sup>ⓔ</sup> Should we continue in sin so that grace may multiply? <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: Absolutely not! <sup>ⓖ</sup> How can we who died to sin <sup>ⓗ</sup> still live in it?
CSB17.3: Or are you unaware that all of us who were baptized <sup>ⓘ</sup> into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death? <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.4: Therefore we were buried with him by baptism into death, <sup>ⓚ</sup> in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead <sup>ⓛ</sup> by the glory of the Father, <sup>ⓜ</sup> so we too may walk in newness <sup>[1]</sup> of life. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.5: For if we have been united with him in the likeness of his death, <sup>ⓐ</sup> we will certainly also be <sup>[2]</sup> in the likeness of his resurrection.
CSB17.6: For we know that our old self <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> was crucified with him <sup>ⓒ</sup> so that the body ruled by sin <sup>[4]</sup> might be rendered powerless <sup>ⓓ</sup> so that we may no longer be enslaved to sin,
CSB17.7: since a person who has died <sup>ⓔ</sup> is freed <sup>[5]</sup> from sin.
CSB17.8: Now if we died with Christ, <sup>ⓕ</sup> we believe that we will also live with him,
CSB17.9: because we know that Christ, having been raised from the dead, <sup>ⓖ</sup> will not die again. Death no longer rules over him. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.10: For the death he died, he died to sin once for all time; but the life he lives, he lives to God.
CSB17.11: So, you too consider yourselves dead to sin <sup>ⓘ</sup> and alive to God in Christ Jesus. <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.12: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body, so that you obey <sup>[7]</sup> its desires.
CSB17.13: And do not offer any parts <sup>[8]</sup> of it to sin <sup>ⓙ</sup> as weapons for unrighteousness. But as those who are alive from the dead, offer yourselves to God, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and all the parts of yourselves to God as weapons for righteousness.
CSB17.14: For sin will not rule over you, because you are not under the law <sup>ⓛ</sup> but under grace. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.15: What then? Should we sin because we are not under the law but under grace? <sup>ⓝ</sup> Absolutely not! <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.16: Don’t you know that if you offer yourselves to someone <sup>[9]</sup> as obedient slaves, <sup>ⓑ</sup> you are slaves of that one you obey <sup>ⓒ</sup>  — either of sin leading to death <sup>ⓓ</sup> or of obedience leading to righteousness?
CSB17.17: But thank God that, although you used to be slaves of sin, <sup>ⓔ</sup> you obeyed from the heart that pattern of teaching to which you were handed <sup>[10]</sup> over, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.18: and having been set free from sin, <sup>ⓖ</sup> you became enslaved to righteousness.
CSB17.19: I am using a human analogy <sup>ⓗ</sup> because of the weakness of your flesh. <sup>[11]</sup> For just as you offered the parts of yourselves as slaves to impurity, and to greater and greater lawlessness, so now offer them as slaves to righteousness, which results in sanctification.
CSB17.20: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free with regard to righteousness. <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.21: So what fruit was produced <sup>[13]</sup> then from the things you are now ashamed of? <sup>ⓙ</sup> The outcome of those things is death. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.22: But now, since you have been set free from sin and have become enslaved to God, <sup>ⓛ</sup> you have your fruit, which results in sanctification <sup>ⓜ</sup>  — and the outcome is eternal life! <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord. <sup>ⓐ</sup>

AMP.1: What shall we say [to all this]? Should we continue in sin and practice sin as a habit so that [God's gift of] grace may increase and overflow?
AMP.2: Certainly not! How can we, the very ones who died to sin, continue to live in it any longer?
AMP.3: Or are you ignorant of the fact that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into His death?
AMP.4: We have therefore been buried with Him through baptism into death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory and power of the Father, we too might walk habitually in newness of life [abandoning our old ways].
AMP.5: For if we have become one with Him [permanently united] in the likeness of His death, we will also certainly be [one with Him and share fully] in the likeness of His resurrection.
AMP.6: We know that our old self [our human nature without the Holy Spirit] was nailed to the cross with Him, in order that our body of sin might be done away with, so that we would no longer be slaves to sin.
AMP.7: For the person who has died [with Christ] has been freed from [the power of] sin.
AMP.8: Now if we have died with Christ, we believe that we will also live [together] with Him,
AMP.9: because we know [the self-evident truth] that Christ, having been raised from the dead, will never die again; death no longer has power over Him.
AMP.10: For the death that He died, He died to sin [ending its power and paying the sinner's debt] once and for all; and the life that He lives, He lives to [glorify] God [in unbroken fellowship with Him].
AMP.11: Even so, consider yourselves to be dead to sin [and your relationship to it broken], but alive to God [in unbroken fellowship with Him] in Christ Jesus.
AMP.12: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its lusts and passions.
AMP.13: Do not go on offering members of your body to sin as instruments of wickedness. But offer yourselves to God [in a decisive act] as those alive [raised] from the dead [to a new life], and your members [all of your abilities—sanctified, set apart] as instruments of righteousness [yielded] to God.
AMP.14: For sin will no longer be a master over you, since you are not under Law [as slaves], but under [unmerited] grace [as recipients of God's favor and mercy].
AMP.15: What then [are we to conclude]? Shall we sin because we are not under Law, but under [God's] grace? Certainly not!
AMP.16: Do you not know that when you continually offer yourselves to someone to do his will, you are the slaves of the one whom you obey, either [slaves] of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness (right standing with God)?
AMP.17: But thank God that though you were slaves of sin, you became obedient with all your heart to the standard of teaching in which you were instructed and to which you were committed.
AMP.18: And having been set free from sin, you have become the slaves of righteousness [of conformity to God's will and purpose].
AMP.19: I am speaking in [familiar] human terms because of your natural limitations [your spiritual immaturity]. For just as you presented your bodily members as slaves to impurity and to [moral] lawlessness, leading to further lawlessness, so now offer your members [your abilities, your talents] as slaves to righteousness, leading to sanctification [that is, being set apart for God's purpose].
AMP.20: When you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness [you had no desire to conform to God's will].
AMP.21: So what benefit did you get at that time from the things of which you are now ashamed? [None!] For the outcome of those things is death!
AMP.22: But now since you have been set free from sin and have become [willing] slaves to God, you have your benefit, resulting in sanctification [being made holy and set apart for God's purpose], and the outcome [of this] is eternal life.
AMP.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God [that is, His remarkable, overwhelming gift of grace to believers] is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

MSG.1: So what do we do? Keep on sinning so God can keep on forgiving?
MSG.2: I should hope not! If we've left the country where sin is sovereign, how can we still live in our old house there?
MSG.3: Or didn't you realize we packed up and left there for good? That is what happened in baptism. When we went under the water, we left the old country of sin behind; when we came up out of the water, we entered into the new country of grace—a new life in a new land! That's what baptism into the life of Jesus means.
MSG.4: When we are lowered into the water, it is like the burial of Jesus; when we are raised up out of the water, it is like the resurrection of Jesus.
MSG.5: Each of us is raised into a light-filled world by our Father so that we can see where we're going in our new grace-sovereign country.
MSG.6: Could it be any clearer? Our old way of life was nailed to the Cross with Christ, a decisive end to that sin-miserable life—no longer at sin's every beck and call! What we believe is this:
MSG.8: If we get included in Christ's sin-conquering death, we also get included in his life-saving resurrection.
MSG.9: We know that when Jesus was raised from the dead it was a signal of the end of death-as-the-end. Never again will death have the last word.
MSG.10: When Jesus died, he took sin down with him, but alive he brings God down to us.
MSG.11: From now on, think of it this way: Sin speaks a dead language that means nothing to you; God speaks your mother tongue, and you hang on every word. You are dead to sin and alive to God. That's what Jesus did.
MSG.12: That means you must not give sin a vote in the way you conduct your lives. Don't give it the time of day.
MSG.13: Don't even run little errands that are connected with that old way of life. Throw yourselves wholeheartedly and full-time—remember, you've been raised from the dead!—into God's way of doing things.
MSG.14: Sin can't tell you how to live. After all, you're not living under that old tyranny any longer. You're living in the freedom of God.
MSG.15: So, since we're out from under the old tyranny, does that mean we can live any old way we want? Since we're free in the freedom of God, can we do anything that comes to mind?
MSG.16: Hardly. You know well enough from your own experience that there are some acts of so-called freedom that destroy freedom. Offer yourselves to sin, for instance, and it's your last free act. But offer yourselves to the ways of God and the freedom never quits. All your lives you've let sin tell you what to do.
MSG.17: But thank God you've started listening to a new master,
MSG.18: one whose commands set you free to live openly in his freedom!
MSG.19: I'm using this freedom language because it's easy to picture. You can readily recall, can't you, how at one time the more you did just what you felt like doing—not caring about others, not caring about God—the worse your life became and the less freedom you had? And how much different is it now as you live in God's freedom, your lives healed and expansive in holiness?
MSG.20: As long as you did what you felt like doing, ignoring God, you didn't have to bother with right thinking or right living, or right anything for that matter.
MSG.21: But do you call that a free life? What did you get out of it? Nothing you're proud of now. Where did it get you? A dead end.
MSG.22: But now that you've found you don't have to listen to sin tell you what to do, and have discovered the delight of listening to God telling you, what a surprise! A whole, healed, put-together life right now, with more and more of life on the way!
MSG.23: Work hard for sin your whole life and your pension is death. But God's gift is real life, eternal life, delivered by Jesus, our Master.

NIV.1:
Dead to Sin, Alive in Christ
What shall we say, then? Shall we go on sinning so that grace may increase?
NIV.2: By no means! We died to sin; how can we live in it any longer?
NIV.3: Or don’t you know that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
NIV.4: We were therefore buried with him through baptism into death in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too may live a new life.
NIV.5: If we have been united with him like this in his death, we will certainly also be united with him in his resurrection.
NIV.6: For we know that our old self was crucified with him so that the body of sin might be done away with, that we should no longer be slaves to sin—
NIV.7: because anyone who has died has been freed from sin.
NIV.8: Now if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
NIV.9: For we know that since Christ was raised from the dead, he cannot die again; death no longer has mastery over him.
NIV.10: The death he died, he died to sin once for all; but the life he lives, he lives to God.
NIV.11: In the same way, count yourselves dead to sin but alive to God in Christ Jesus.
NIV.12: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its evil desires.
NIV.13: Do not offer the parts of your body to sin, as instruments of wickedness, but rather offer yourselves to God, as those who have been brought from death to life; and offer the parts of your body to him as instruments of righteousness.
NIV.14: For sin shall not be your master, because you are not under law, but under grace.
NIV.15:
Slaves to Righteousness
What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!
NIV.16: Don’t you know that when you offer yourselves to someone to obey him as slaves, you are slaves to the one whom you obey—whether you are slaves to sin, which leads to death, or to obedience, which leads to righteousness?
NIV.17: But thanks be to God that, though you used to be slaves to sin, you wholeheartedly obeyed the form of teaching to which you were entrusted.
NIV.18: You have been set free from sin and have become slaves to righteousness.
NIV.19: I put this in human terms because you are weak in your natural selves. Just as you used to offer the parts of your body in slavery to impurity and to ever-increasing wickedness, so now offer them in slavery to righteousness leading to holiness.
NIV.20: When you were slaves to sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
NIV.21: What benefit did you reap at that time from the things you are now ashamed of? Those things result in death!
NIV.22: But now that you have been set free from sin and have become slaves to God, the benefit you reap leads to holiness, and the result is eternal life.
NIV.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

NRSVCE.1: What then are we to say? Should we continue in sin in order that grace may abound?
NRSVCE.2: By no means! How can we who died to sin go on living in it?
NRSVCE.3: Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
NRSVCE.4: Therefore we have been buried with him by baptism into death, so that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, so we too might walk in newness of life.
NRSVCE.5: For if we have been united with him in a death like his, we will certainly be united with him in a resurrection like his.
NRSVCE.6: We know that our old self was crucified with him so that the body of sin might be destroyed, and we might no longer be enslaved to sin.
NRSVCE.7: For whoever has died is freed from sin.
NRSVCE.8: But if we have died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
NRSVCE.9: We know that Christ, being raised from the dead, will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him.
NRSVCE.10: The death he died, he died to sin, once for all; but the life he lives, he lives to God.
NRSVCE.11: So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.12: Therefore, do not let sin exercise dominion in your mortal bodies, to make you obey their passions.
NRSVCE.13: No longer present your members to sin as instruments of wickedness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life, and present your members to God as instruments of righteousness.
NRSVCE.14: For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace.
NRSVCE.15: What then? Should we sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!
NRSVCE.16: Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness?
NRSVCE.17: But thanks be to God that you, having once been slaves of sin, have become obedient from the heart to the form of teaching to which you were entrusted,
NRSVCE.18: and that you, having been set free from sin, have become slaves of righteousness.
NRSVCE.19: I am speaking in human terms because of your natural limitations. For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and to greater and greater iniquity, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness for sanctification.
NRSVCE.20: When you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
NRSVCE.21: So what advantage did you then get from the things of which you now are ashamed? The end of those things is death.
NRSVCE.22: But now that you have been freed from sin and enslaved to God, the advantage you get is sanctification. The end is eternal life.
NRSVCE.23: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

NET.1: What shall we say then? Are we to remain in sin so that grace may increase?
NET.2: Absolutely not! How can we who died to sin still live in it?
NET.3: Or do you not know that as many as were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
NET.4: Therefore we have been buried with him through baptism into death, in order that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, so we too may live a new life.
NET.5: For if we have become united with him in the likeness of his death, we will certainly also be united in the likeness of his resurrection.
NET.6: We know that our old man was crucified with him so that the body of sin would no longer dominate us, so that we would no longer be enslaved to sin.
NET.7: (For someone who has died has been freed from sin.)
NET.8: Now if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
NET.9: We know that since Christ has been raised from the dead, he is never going to die again; death no longer has mastery over him.
NET.10: For the death he died, he died to sin once for all, but the life he lives, he lives to God.
NET.11: So you too consider yourselves dead to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus.
NET.12: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its desires,
NET.13: and do not present your members to sin as instruments to be used for unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God as those who are alive from the dead and your members to God as instruments to be used for righteousness.
NET.14: For sin will have no mastery over you, because you are not under law but under grace.
NET.15: What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? Absolutely not!
NET.16: Do you not know that if you present yourselves as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one you obey, either of sin resulting in death, or obedience resulting in righteousness?
NET.17: But thanks be to God that though you were slaves to sin, you obeyed from the heart that pattern of teaching you were entrusted to,
NET.18: and having been freed from sin, you became enslaved to righteousness.
NET.19: (I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh.) For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and lawlessness leading to more lawlessness, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness leading to sanctification.
NET.20: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free with regard to righteousness.
NET.21: So what benefit did you then reap from those things that you are now ashamed of? For the end of those things is death.
NET.22: But now, freed from sin and enslaved to God, you have your benefit leading to sanctification, and the end is eternal life.
NET.23: For the payoff of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
